div.p: Let it be known to all who shall inspect the present written deed that I, William Comyn, and my heirs, have given and conceded to God and to Saint Cuthbert, for the light of the church of Saint Cuthbert, in perpetual alms, one stone of wax to be rendered annually at the feast of Saint Cuthbert in September. And so that this donation remain established and steadfast, I, William Comyn, have strengthened it by the application of my seal. With these witnesses: Richard my son and heir, David Graham, Roger the parson of Rule, and many others.
